vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1718: Duplicate successful 'pan reload' restart-status writes from two unidentified concurrent processes

plan.title: Duplicate successful 'pan reload' restart-status writes from two unidentified concurrent processes
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: One physical dashboard restart produced two successful trigger='pan reload' writes to restart-status.json from two processes, despite a fail-fast restart lock. Provable cause: pan reload's `pan dev` SIGUSR2 branch (reload.ts:94-111) records success=true and returns BEFORE acquireRestartLock (reload.ts:113), bypassing mutual exclusion. The single-entry restart-status.json also makes it impossible to identify a second concurrent writer after the fact (last-write-wins). A dead supervisor.pid in the same window points at a supervisor stop/start cycle that left a pidfile pointing at a process that died on EADDRINUSE.
narratives.Proposal: (1) Acquire the restart lock at the top of reloadCommand, before the dev-marker branch, so every reload path is mutually excluded or visibly fails. (2) Append every restart-status write to an append-only restart-events.jsonl journal and add a pure concurrent-writer detector. (3) Surface the restart writer's pid/initiator and a concurrent-writer warning in `pan status`. (4) Make the supervisor stop/start cycle wait for actual death and verify child survival so it never leaves a dead pidfile.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT redesign the supervisor supervision/health/watchdog model — only make stop wait-for-exit and start verify-survival.
- Do NOT change restart-status.json's single-entry last-write-wins contract; the journal is additive.
- Do NOT add a distributed/OS-level lock; the existing fail-fast advisory restart lock is retained.
- Do NOT claim a definitive proven mechanism for the original two long writes; the journal is the means to identify it on next occurrence.
- Do NOT alter the restart announcer's polling/announce behavior beyond what reading existing fields requires.

autoDecisions.summary: Acquire the restart lock at the very top of reloadCommand, before the pan dev SIGUSR2 branch, releasing it in a single try/finally around both paths.
autoDecisions.rationale: It is the one provable lock bypass and directly satisfies acceptance criterion 2 (concurrent flows excluded or visibly fail). Minimal, surgical, single-file.

autoDecisions.summary: Wire the append-only journal INSIDE writeRestartStatusPromise rather than at each of the four call sites.
autoDecisions.rationale: DRY and guarantees all writers (reload, restart, watchdog x2) are captured with no risk of a forgotten call site; matches the single-source-of-truth tenet for the writer.

autoDecisions.summary: Include the supervisor stop/start robustness fix (Finding C) in this issue rather than deferring it.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue body presents the dead supervisor.pid as part of the same concurrent-restart-flow root cause; fixing it closes the corroborating symptom. Scoped tightly via NonGoals to avoid a supervisor redesign.

autoDecisions.summary: Do not enforce a 'must hold the lock to write success' invariant on writeRestartStatus.
autoDecisions.rationale: The watchdog legitimately writes status after releasing the lock, and failure-status writes by a blocked process are valid; such an invariant would break correct flows. The lock-on-restart plus append-journal is the right model.

items.title: Acquire the restart lock before the pan reload dev-marker SIGUSR2 branch

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/reload.ts, restructure reloadCommand so acquireRestartLock('pan reload') runs immediately after the health-timeout parse and BEFORE the `pan dev` marker check (currently lines 94-111, before the lock at line 113). Wrap the dev-marker branch and the detached-restart branch in a single try { ... } finally { await lock.release(); }. If the lock is not acquired, record success=false with 'restart in progress (held by ...)' and exit 2 WITHOUT reading the dev marker, signalling SIGUSR2, or restarting. On the dev path with the lock held: send SIGUSR2, recordReloadStatus(true), and return (lock released by finally). Keep the existing detached-path behavior byte-for-byte otherwise.

items.title: Given a `pan dev` marker is present AND the restart lock is already held, when `pan reload` runs, then it records success=false (trigger='pan reload'), does NOT call process.kill(...SIGUSR2), and exits with code 2.

Given a `pan dev` marker is present AND the lock is free, when `pan reload` runs, then it acquires the lock, sends SIGUSR2 to the dev pid, records success=true, and releases the lock (existing PAN-1662 test stays green and additionally asserts acquireRestartLock was called).

Given no dev marker and the lock is held by another process, when `pan reload` runs, then it records success=false 'restart in progress' and exits 2 without running bun install or the build (existing detached-path behavior preserved).
Append every restart-status write to an append-only restart-events.jsonl journal

narrative.Action: In src/lib/restart-status.ts: after the existing tmp+rename of restart-status.json inside writeRestartStatusPromise, append one JSON line (the same RestartStatus entry) to `${getOverdeckHome()}/restart-events.jsonl` using appendFile (O_APPEND). Make the append best-effort (a journal failure must not fail the primary status write or throw). Add readRestartEvents(limit?: number): Promise<RestartStatus[]> that reads the journal, parses each line (skipping malformed lines), and returns the last `limit` entries. Add a pure detectConcurrentRestartWriters(events: RestartStatus[], windowMs = 60000): RestartStatus[] that returns the set of successful entries whose ts fall within windowMs of each other but carry different pids (empty when none). Bound journal growth to the last ~200 entries (trim on append when the line count exceeds the cap). Export Effect variants consistent with the file's existing pattern.

items.title: Given two writeRestartStatus calls in sequence, when the journal is read with readRestartEvents(), then it returns BOTH entries in order while restart-status.json still contains only the last entry.

Given two successful entries 2.6s apart with different pids, when detectConcurrentRestartWriters() runs, then it returns both entries; given the same pid or entries >60s apart, then it returns an empty array.
Given the journal already holds 200 lines, when another entry is appended, then the file is trimmed to the most recent 200 entries.
Given the journal path is unwritable, when writeRestartStatus runs, then restart-status.json is still written successfully and no error propagates to the caller.
Surface the restart writer (pid + initiator) and concurrent-writer warning in pan status

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/status.ts, extract the restart line construction into a pure exported helper formatRestartStatusLines(status: RestartStatus | null, events: RestartStatus[]): string[] that (a) appends `pid <n>` and, when present, the initiator to the existing 'Last dashboard restart' line, and (b) when detectConcurrentRestartWriters(events) is non-empty, adds a yellow warning line naming each concurrent writer's pid and initiator. Have statusCommand call readRestartEvents() and pass the result in. Add a new test file src/cli/commands/__tests__/status-restart.test.ts covering the pure helper (no status harness needed).

items.title: Given a restart-status with pid=1733121 and initiator='conv-20260610-573d', when `pan status` renders, then the restart line includes 'pid 1733121' and the initiator.
Given the journal contains two successful pan reload entries 2.6s apart with different pids, when formatRestartStatusLines runs, then it emits a warning line naming both pids.
Given no concurrent writers in the journal, when formatRestartStatusLines runs, then no warning line is emitted and the existing single-line format is preserved.
Supervisor stop waits for exit; start verifies survival — no dead pidfile

narrative.Action: In src/lib/supervisor.ts: (1) stopSupervisorProcessSync — after SIGTERM, poll isProcessAlive(pid) with a bounded sync wait (total ~2s; escalate to SIGKILL at ~1.5s) BEFORE unlinking the pidfile and returning, so the stop actually takes effect. (2) startSupervisorProcessSync — after writeFileSync(pid), wait ~150ms (sync) and re-check isProcessAlive(child.pid); if the child has already died (e.g. EADDRINUSE against the still-running old supervisor), log a clear error and unlink the pidfile so no dead pid is left behind. Use a sync sleep via Atomics.wait(new Int32Array(new SharedArrayBuffer(4)), 0, 0, ms) — never a busy-spin and never execSync('sleep').

items.title: Given a live supervisor pid, when stopSupervisorProcessSync runs, then it does not unlink the pidfile or return until isProcessAlive(pid) is false (or the SIGKILL-escalation bound elapses).
Given startSupervisorProcessSync spawns a child that dies immediately, when start completes, then it logs an error and leaves NO pidfile pointing at the dead pid.
Given the bounded sync waits, when reviewed, then they use Atomics.wait (not a busy-loop and not execSync) and total stop-wait never exceeds ~2s.
